steps.title: Recognize the hypothesis as comparative
steps.content:
The hypothesis makes a comparison: in low-visibility conditions, wider-headed fish will be less likely than narrower-headed fish to detect obstructions.

To directly support this prediction, evidence must show two fish with different head shapes tested under the same conditions—with the wider-headed one detecting fewer obstacles.

steps.title: Match the comparison to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B directly compares two species with different head shapes: the wider-headed bristlemouth bumped into obstructions more often than the narrower-headed dusky smooth-hound in the specified test conditions. This demonstrates exactly what the hypothesis predicts.

The others lack this comparative structure: one shows only one head shape without comparison, one shows equal performance, and one examines within-species variation.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
